title: PPIC PIL CKW
summary: A PPIC-focused web system for the CKW (Cikawung) region handling stage-1 production — converting raw materials into usable materials — with HRIS integration for workforce and R&D for planning new item types. Outputs transit to CKP (Cikampek) for SO-driven processing.

sections.title: Context
paragraphs: Built for the PPIC division in CKW to manage stage-1 production: converting raw materials into usable materials that are shipped to CKP (Cikampek) for Sales Order–driven processing.
Includes a workforce (HRIS) facet and R&D support for planning and creating new item types.
Due to confidentiality, only a subset of screenshots are shown.

sections.title: Scope & Modules
bullets: Master data: items, machines, usage records, on‑going machines.
Machine map/visualization to reflect real‑time utilization.
HRIS: workforce management and assignment to lines/stations.
R&D: plan and trial new item types before operationalization.

sections.title: Technical Decisions
bullets: Next.js + React + TypeScript for fast UI iteration and SSR where helpful.
TanStack Query for data fetching/caching and optimistic UX.
Ant Design for consistent, accessible UI components.
Prisma for schema modeling and type‑safe data access.
Bull queues for background processing and long‑running jobs.
WebSockets for live machine status and production updates.
Multer and Nodemailer for uploads and event notifications.
REST API with Express/Node.js for service boundaries.

sections.title: Outcomes
paragraphs: Streamlined production coordination, clearer machine utilization, and tighter HRIS/R&D alignment.

title: PMS - Sales Performance Management
summary: A web platform for sales performance management with interactive dashboards and streamlined workflows. Covers PR→PO procurement, customer PO, quotations and pricing, Sales Orders with recommended auto-prices, lead management, and end‑to‑end SO tracking.

sections.title: Context
paragraphs: Designed to give operations and commercial teams real‑time visibility of sales productivity, pipeline health, and order fulfillment—from lead to invoice.
Consolidates pricing logic, quotations, orders, and procurement (PR→PO) to reduce manual handoffs and improve SLA adherence.

sections.title: Scope & Modules
bullets: Leads & Marketing: capture sources, qualify leads, and progress opportunities.
Sales Quotation & Quotation Pricing: generate quotes with recommended price baselines; manual adjustments supported with audit trails.
Sales Order (SO): create SOs from approved quotes; automatic price suggestions based on strategy and cost inputs.
SO Tracking: end‑to‑end status, fulfillment progress, and exceptions.
Master Data: items, customers, pricing rules, sales teams/territories.
Procurement: PR→PO workflow for purchasing materials and services.
Customer PO: register and reconcile customer POs against quotations/SOs.

sections.title: Technical Decisions
bullets: Next.js + React + TypeScript for fast, type‑safe iteration and SSR/ISR where helpful.
TanStack Query for caching, revalidation, and optimistic UX.
Ant Design for consistent, accessible UI with fast CRUD scaffolding.
Prisma for schema modeling and type‑safe DB access; REST API with Express/Node.js.
Bull for background jobs (price recomputations, email notifications, document generation).
WebSockets for live updates on SO status, procurement state, and dashboards.
Multer for uploads (supporting attachments on PR/PO/Quote); Nodemailer for approval flows.

sections.title: Outcomes
paragraphs: Centralized sales pipeline and order tracking improved visibility and reduced cross‑team coordination friction.
Automated price recommendations shortened quotation turnaround and standardized pricing practices.
Streamlined PR→PO and customer PO handling reduced manual errors and sped up fulfillment.
